trait.name: Description
trait.text:
The origins of Ironwrought are many and varied - they can be the artisanal masterpiece of a Golemsmith, or perhaps a relic of a mass production of an ancient lost civilization... or perhaps not-so-lost a civilization. Their source and purpose will vary greatly. Were they made with sapience in mind, or did they gain it later? Were they made for labor or war? Some Ironwrought will dwell on these questions and what it means for them to exist outside of the boundary of their original purpose, but many simply move on, acquiring new parameters to live their life from those they interact with, finding their purpose in life as any of their more fleshy counterparts might. How they are treated in society will vary based on how common they are. In settings where Ironwrought created for labor are common, they will generally be treated quite neutrally, while they may be treated with greater fear or concern if Ironwrought were primarily created as war machines, though in both cases they might be mistaken for their last sapient cousins.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation
Source: Kibbles Compendium of Craft and Creation (Third Party)

trait.name: Self Aware
trait.text: While most constructs are not truly self aware, an Ironwrought is a special case of a cosntruct that has attained a full degree of self-awareness... sometimes in a more rudimentary way, sometimes as self-aware as any human. How that occurred is often dependent on your origin. Here is a table of some origins of your self-awareness. You can roll on the following table, pick one that suits your character, or select a different reason entirely based on your game's setting and character's backstory.
Origin of Sapience
d8 | Origin
1 | You existed without self awareness for years before a magical accident rendered you self aware.
2 | You were a new experiment, designed for more complicated tasks, and ended up more sapient than expected
3 | Left to a mindless task for hundreds of years in a long abandoned location, you slowly developed a sense of self until you abandoned your programmed task.
4 | Your sapience is the result of the influence of a supernatural being (celestial, fey, or even fiend) granting you self-awareness for their purposes (or entertainment).
5 | You awoke from a long hibernation changed, with greater self awareness. You don't remember the past.
6 | The Inventor that created intentionally granted you full sapience, trying to build a child, friend, or adventuring companion.
7 | You are the consciousness of a mortal imbued into a metal shell, transferred to a Construct to save your life.
8 | You are the inventor that created yourself, transferring your consciousness to the Construct for reasons of your own.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Age
trait.text: Constructs do not age physically, though their mental abilities may change greatly through their life as they process new information and experiences.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Alignment
trait.text: Ironwrought tend to be inclined toward Lawful Neutral, following an internal logic and purpose, but player Ironwrought have gained full sapiance, and can make their own decisions. Some opt to rebel entirely against their nature, acting in unpredictable chaotic patterns like any mortals.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Size
trait.text: Medium
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Speed
trait.text: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Living Construct
trait.text: You are immune to disease. You do not need to eat, drink, or breathe. You do not need sleep, and magic can't put you to sleep.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and one other language of your choice (usually the language of your creator if they spoke a language other than common).
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Power Down
trait.text: You do not need to sleep. When you take a long rest, you must spend at least 4 hours during a long rest in a motionless lower power state. While in this state, you have disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks, but remain aware of your surroundings.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Construct
trait.text: Your type is construct.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Arcane Life
trait.text: You can absorb certain types of healing magic, allowing you to recover hit points from magical effects that do not otherwise affect constructs (such as the cure wounds spell).
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Modular Design
trait.text: You can select two of the following specialized traits representing the functionality built into your chassis.
• Integrated Armor. You gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class.

• Integrated Weapon. You have a simple or martial weapon of your choice built into your frame. You gain proficiency with this weapon. This does not remove the need for ammunition for weapons that have the ammunition property. This weapon is typically integrated into your limbs, and requires hands to use as normal.

• Language Module. You learn one additional language, and can cast comprehend languages without consuming a spell slot. Once you cast this spell, you cannot cast it again until you finish a long rest. You can cast this spell using spell slots.
• Natural Armor. You have built in armor, giving you a base AC of 16 (your Dexterity modifier doesn't affect this number). You can use a shield and apply the shield's bonus as normal.
• Night Mode. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.
• Rapid Movement. Your base walking speed increases by 5 feet.
• Redundant Logic. You process all inputs twice to ensure they are logical. You have advantage on saving throws against becoming frightened or charmed.
• Specialized Skill. You gain proficiency in one skill of your choice.
• Specialized Tool. You gain one tool built into your frame. You gain proficiency with this tool.
• Terrain Adaptation . You gain a climbing or swimming speed equal to your movement speed.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Chassis Configuration
trait.text: Ironwrought come in many shapes and sizes. Select one of the following configurations of your chassis.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Humanoid
trait.text: Your shape is roughly that of a bipedal humanoid. You gain the following traits.
Adaptable Design. You can make one additional selection from your modular design trait.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Quadrupedal
trait.text: Your shape has four legs and no hands. You gain the following traits.

Quadrupedal. You do not have hands. You can hold one object or grapple one thing using your mouth, but cannot speak when you do so, and any weapons held in this way are wielded with disadvantage. You can perform the somatic components of spells with one of your legs, however doing so requires 15 feet of your movement.

Natural Weapons. You gain a selection of natural weapons. You are considered to be holding natural weapons for the purposes of abilities that require you to to hold a weapon (this does not confer disadvantage to them). Select two of the following:
• Metal Chompers. You have metal fangs suitable for chomping. On hit, they deal 1d10 piercing damage.
• Razor Claws. You have two sets of razor claws integrated into your front limbs. On hit, they deal 1d6 slashing damage and have the light property.
• Bladed Tail. You have a lashing bladed tail. On hit, it deals 1d4 slashing damage. It has the reach property.
Barding. You can only wear armor specially adapted to your unique shape. This custom armor has the same price as normal armor, but may be harder to find or more expensive when custom ordered.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Variant Large Constructs
trait.text: At the discretion of your GM, a quadrupedal Ironwrought construct can be large sized. It gains the following traits:

Large Natural Weapons. The damage dice of your natural weapons increases by one size (from a d10 to a d12 or from a d6 to a d8). Your natural weapons without the light property gain the heavy property.
Bulwark. You count as three quarters cover for allied creatures when you would normally count as half cover.
Barding. You cannot wear armor designed for normal sized creatures. Your armor has to be designed for large sized creatures of your form, and generally costs four times the normal cost of armor.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Description
trait.text:
Farlings are a race that comes from beyond the material plane, though few are certain where. While humanoid in shape, they are immediately distinguished from mortal races. They have a slender build, large eyes, and tendrils in place of hair. Their strange nature and inherent psionic abilities make them easily feared, but they tend to be far more friendly to mortal races than most of that which comes from the beyond. Farlings tend to be blue to purple, but can be more exotic colors, or even have strange and exotic colorations and patterns in some cases. The nature and location of their creation results in little consistency of exact appearance, though they will often have some traits of their progenitor (such as sharing an eye or skin coloration).
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation
Source: Kibbles Compendium of Craft and Creation (Third Party)

trait.name: Adaptable Mind
trait.text: Farlings are exceedingly adept at picking up social cues and cultural norms, and despite their strange appearance can easily fit into a tolerant society, but often maintain exceedingly strange senses of humor and behavior, fully aware of its oddity, sometimes even playing up their alien nature. They enjoy seeing the reactions of other creatures to strange circumstances, even if they have to cause those situations.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Memory and Aging
trait.text: Farlings age by gaining memories. They do not biologically age, but have the ability to make permanent crystalized memories, and age roughly one day each time they do. Young Farlings make these memories a dozen times a day, aging rapidly while absorbing new information at an incredible rate, while aging Farlings become more selective in what they choose to remember. If they do not commit some memory in this way, their memory tends to be still somewhat better than a human, but they view these memories as transient things that will be eventually forgotten.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Nature and Biology
trait.text: Farlings do not have a gender, though those that travel among other races may associate with one. They have a generally androgynous and alien apparence. They are capable of forming strong emotional bonds, though some (particularly younger ones) will find the idea of gender and the associated customs hilarious (potentially inappropriately so based on the culture). Farlings that exist for hundreds of years and absorb thousands of memories can create a new Farling by traveling to realms of mind and thought where reality is more flexible, and psionically willing a new one into existence.

A newly created Farling is often left to experience the world on their own shortly after the creation, left with associates or friends of its creator (that they have hopefully made arrangements with) who will then move on, seeking to let the new Farling develop on its own, often seeking to not influence their experiences more than necessary.

Taking care of a young Farling can be exhausting due to the rampant curiosity and inquisitive nature rapidly consuming new experiences and memories, but their inquisitive nature causes them to age exceedingly quickly. A young Farling can fully absorb many memories day, aging exceedingly fast. It is usually not long before they seek to wander broader environments, and set out into the world.
Farlings are not aquatic, but are unusually suited to it, often finding water quite comfortable and are natural swimmers.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Age
trait.text: A Farling ages by absorbing memories. Each time they permanently absorb a memory, they age roughly one day. A young Farling can absorb dozens of memories a day, aging rapidly. Once reaching adulthood (a process that often only takes a decade), the rate at which they can absorb memories begins to slow, they become more selective, aging far slower.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Alignment
trait.text: Farlings show little consistency in alignment, and are shaped by their circumstances and background. Their curious and impulsive nature often leads younger members of the race to exhibit chaotic tendencies.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Size
trait.text: Most Farlings stand between 5 and 6 feet tall, with a slighter build than humans. Your size is Medium.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Speed
trait.text: Your base walking speed is 30 feet. You have a swimming speed of 20 feet.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Aberration
trait.text: Your creature type is Aberration, rather than humanoid.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Languages
trait.text: You can speak, read, and write Common, Deep Speech, and one extra language of your choice.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Adaptive Intuition
trait.text: You gain proficiency in Insight, and have advantage on Wisdom (Insight) ability checks to determine social cues or cultural customs.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Psionic Mind
trait.text: You gain the Telepathy Discipline of the psion, though cannot use the Alternate Effects unless you gain this Discipline from another source. If you gain this Discipline from another source, you gain 1 additional psi point instead.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Crystalized Memory
trait.text: When you learn or experience something, you can fix that memory, making it a permanent memory you can always remember with crystal clarity. This memory can be anything that occurs within a 1 hour span (it can be as short as a fleeting sensation, or as long as 1 hour of events).

Once you do this, you cannot do so again until you complete a long rest. Absorbing these memories is how a Farling ages, and you age one day for each memory you absorb in this way. You can absorb a memory from a willing creature via your telepathy in this way.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Broad Horizons
trait.text: You have resistance to psychic damage. Once per day, if take psychic damage from a critical hit, you can choose to absorb that damage as a memory, taking no damage.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Skill Absorption
trait.text: Your rapid ability to learn new skills grants you proficiency in one artisan tool and one skill of your choice. At your GMs discretion, if your game allows for the training in tools and skills, you may gain proficiency in them twice as fast using your Crystalized Memory feature daily.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Variant Farling
trait.text: At the discrection of your GM, the following is an intended feature of Farlings that can be added in place of Skill Absorption. This is a recommended feature to allow additional options, but may not be suited for all games due to interacterations with other custom rules at some tables:

Alien Mind. When you gain the Spellcasting or Psionics ability from any class or feature, you can change the associated ability score to your choice of Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma. Any time that class references the previous spell casting ability, you can replace it with your selected score (for example, if you are playing a Psion and change its associated ability score to Charisma, Empowered Psiosnics would add Charisma instead of Intelligence).
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Description
trait.text:
The augmented are creatures that have replaced parts of the body with mechanical and magical parts - some of the an arcane cyborg. The origin of these parts is many and varied, as is their nature - anything from living magic to mechanical means is possible, based on the setting and character. These provide bonuses that replace or augment the default features of your race selection.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation
Source: Kibbles Compendium of Craft and Creation (Third Party)

trait.name: Intentionally Created
trait.text: Almost all augmented are intentionally created - not always be the intentions of the individual, but almost always by the intention of someone. This often leads open the question of why, and for what purpose.
Many of them are created for war, some as bizare experiments, some to restore missing pieces, sometimes they had a choice in the process, and some will insist that they never asked for it.
Quirks
d4 | Quirks
1 | You tinker with yourself and collect spare parts.
2 | When you allies fail at things, you suggest what augments may help the succeed in the future.
3 | You decorate your augmented parts with guady decorations.
4 | You are afraid of getting wet, and terrified of large bodies of water.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Modified Origin
trait.text: Select an existing race option. You gain the creature type, size, and languages of that race.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Ability Score Increase
trait.text: Your Constitution score increases by 1, and one ability score of your choice increases by 1.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Aligmment
trait.text: An augmented creature can be any alignment, but have a inclination toward lawful alignments.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Speed
trait.text: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Inherited Abilities
trait.text: You can keep the following elements of that race: any skill proficiencies you gained from it and any climbing, flying, or swimming speed you gained from it. Alternatively, you can forgo this to select two Augmented Abilities (below).
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Augmented Abilites
trait.text: On top of your inherited abilties, you have been augmented with one of the following, granting you special new abilities:

• Arcane Arm. Your arm is replaced by a mechanical or magical replacement. Your Strength score increases by 1, and it can serve as a natural weapon. It deals 1d6 bludgeoning damage on hit, and counts as a simple weapon with the light property.

• Arcane Eye. Your eye (one or both) are replaced by mechanical or magical replacements. You can see in dim light within 120 feet of you as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light. You discern colors in that darkness as shades of gray, and gain proficiency in the Perception skill. You can cast the detect magic spell, and once you reach 5th level, the see invisible spell this eye. Casting them in this way requires no material components. Once you cast either of these spells with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast these spells using any spell slots you have.

• Arcane Heart. Your heart (or equivalient organ) is replaced by a mechanical or magical replacement. Your Constitution score increases by 1, and whenever you roll hit dice, you gain temporary hit points equal to the number of hit dice expended.

• Arcane Mind. Your mind is enhanced by mechanical or magical minds. Your Intelligence score increases by 1, and you gain advantage on any Intelligence skill check to recall information you've seen seen before, solve mathmatical equations, detect patterns, or break ciphers and codes
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Description
trait.text:
The warped are members of races that have been deeply influenced by the powers from beyond, twisting their mind and body with mysterious power that has left them fundamentally changed.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation
Source: Kibbles Compendium of Craft and Creation (Third Party)

trait.name: Twisted Origin
trait.text: Most often this comes from long exposure, sometimes only manifesting in creatures born under its influence, but there are those that have been warped later in life, generally by more direct interactions with the creatures and that lurk beyond the veil of sanity.

Many of the warped fall to madness, unable to escape the whispering voices and strange visions, but ones that become adventurers have managed to learn to live with their condition, and consequently have minds of steel.
Quirks
d4 | Quirks
1 | You hide your warped nature under wrappings
2 | You like to show off your enhanced abilities.
3 | Your eyes and hair are unnatural colors.
4 | You mutter to yourself frequently.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Warped Origin
trait.text: Select an existing race option. You gain the creature type, size, and languages of that race.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Whispers of Madness
trait.text: You have learned to understanding the whispering voices you hear. You learn Deep Speech.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Ability Score Increase
trait.text: Your Constitution score increases by 1, and your Intelligence score increases by 2.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Aligmment
trait.text: An augmented creature can be any alignment, but have a inclination toward chaotic alignments.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Speed
trait.text: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Innurred of Madness
trait.text: Things that cause madness in others seem relatively benign to you. You have resistance to psychic damage. You automatically pass any saves against madness.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Inner Voice
trait.text: You have an inner voice that does not belong to you, frequently muttering nonsense, but occasionally offering useful advice, letting you add 1d6 to any ability check you make. You can do this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all uses at the end of a long rest.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation

trait.name: Warped Gift
trait.text: Your condition grants you one special trait. Some may view it as a curse or burden, but you've adapted to using it to your advantage. Select one of the following options:

• Warped Eyes. You see the world in a different way. You have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks against living creatures, and disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks against non-living things (such as traps or details in your environment).
• Warped Limb. One of your limbs is abnormally long and flexible. Any weapon wielded in that hand gains the reach property.

• Warped Mind. Your mind is a foreign and alien place compared to other humanoids. You are immune to magic that allows other creatures to read your thoughts, determine whether you are lying, know your alignment, or know your creature type. Creatures can telepathically communicate with you only if you allow it.
• Warped Skin. Your skin is a rough mottled hide. You have resistance to acid damage.

• Warped Form. Your body is more malleable and flexible than a normal humanoid. You can move through spaces for a smaller creatures without squeezing and when you aren't wearing armor, your AC is 12 + your Constitution modifier. You can use your natural armor to determine your AC if the armor you wear would leave you with a lower AC. A shield's benefits apply as normal while you use your natural armor.
Kibbles' Compendium of Craft and Creation
